title: NYC City Planning — Shared Geography Spine
description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions for the New York City Department of City Planning (DCP). DCP is the SOURCE of the city's shared geography — the BBL (via PLUTO/MapPLUTO), community districts, Neighborhood Tabulation Areas (NTAs), census tracts, council/election district boundaries, and borough boundaries that every other NYC agency references. These definitions are therefore AUTHORITATIVE, not borrowed: they are the base the cross-domain `nyc-commons/geography.json` + `nyc-commons/identifiers.json` schema set (see ../../ROADMAP.md) should be factored FROM, and that Parks (`gisPropNum`), DOE (`DBN`), Council (`matterId`), and BOE poll sites all implicitly carry. Reconciled from the PLUTO (64uk-42ks, 108 cols), NTA (9nt8-h7nd), Community Districts (xi7c-iiu2), and Census Tracts (63ge-mke6) datasets, and the DCP GeoService / GeoClient geocoder.

x-nyc-commons.note: DCP defines these identifiers and boundaries; nyc-commons should $ref DCP as the canonical origin of the geography spine rather than re-declaring it.

Borough.description: One of the five NYC boroughs.

BoroCode.description: DCP borough code: 1=Manhattan, 2=Bronx, 3=Brooklyn, 4=Queens, 5=Staten Island. The first digit of every BBL/BIN.

BBL.description: Borough-Block-Lot — the 10-digit citywide tax-lot key (1 borough digit + 5 block + 4 lot). DCP's PLUTO/MapPLUTO is the authoritative source of the BBL. This is the primary geographic join key across NYC datasets.

BIN.description: Building Identification Number — 7-digit building key (leading digit = borocode). Assigned by DCP; joins buildings to lots.

CommunityDistrict.description: A NYC Community District (Community Board area). DCP-defined; e.g. 'MN01' / borocd 101.

CouncilDistrict.description: City Council district (1–51). Boundaries are published by DCP (the geography every Council dataset joins on).

CensusTract.description: 2020 US Census tract as maintained by DCP (63ge-mke6).

NTA.description: Neighborhood Tabulation Area (2020 vintage) — DCP-defined aggregation of census tracts used for neighborhood-level demographics.

GeographySpine.description: The shared geographic-identity block DCP attaches to a place, and the set of keys other agencies reference. Present (implicitly) on Parks facilities, DOE schools, Council funding, BOE poll sites. Make this the nyc-commons base.
